children beneficiaries and ultimately contribute to the success of the program as a
whole.
Likewise, the Sanggunian Bayan has to expedite the resolution for the purchase of
goods for the implementation of the SFP. The Local Chief Executive ensure that all
the undertakings and preparations are made necessary for the SFP implementation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to
publicize the projects/programs/activities in conspicuous places within the
agency premises and in the project venue or notify the public on its essential
features/elements in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004
dated January 30, 2014.
Moreover, the Municipal Engineer require the contractor/s in their on-going and
future projects to install safety signages in the construction sites and provide
protective gears to the laborers. Finally, explain/justify the non-installation of
project billboards and signboards and the failure to provide safety and health
items, despite the availability of the fund and require the contractor to make a
refund of the said amount.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to
ensure that detailed engineering investigations, surveys and designs are
sufficiently carried out for infrastructure projects to guarantee the completion of
the project.
Moreover, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that
government funds and property were used efficiently and economically and for
the benefit of the public. The Municipal Planning and Development Officer and the
Municipal Engineer properly plan the ensuing construction work and utilize the
completed multipurpose gymnasium. Similarly, submit explanation/justification
why this situation happened.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Agriculturist to
assist the Barangay Officials to re-establish and operate again their MRFs and
allot funding for this purpose in compliance with Section Rule XI of the IRR of RA
No. 9003 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2009-168.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor take the necessary actions to
implement the plans and programs embodied in the approved Ten Year Solid
Waste Management Plan and submit status report of the proposed sanitary landfill
to the audit team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the following:
 The Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the liquidation reports if
not yet submitted and the check to the source agency for refund of the
unexpended balance. See to it that future fund transfers are fully
implemented within the time frame to avoid delay in liquidation. As to the
balance of fund transfers of completed projects with counterpart from the
Municipality, identify the amounts to be remitted to the source agency and
to be reverted in the General Fund for appropriation.
 The Head of the Municipal Planning and Development Office to coordinate
with the concerned department heads to enforce the utilization and
implementation of those projects that were not yet implemented while
pursue continuation of those ongoing utilization. Propose projects and
activities that can be considered as valid activities for the utilization of
those projects. Likewise, see to it that their respective time frames and
schedules are strictly monitored and evaluated to avoid delays in its
implementation.

We recommended that the Municipality allocate 1 percent of its IRA to establish
the Local Councils for the Protection of Children in compliance with DILG
Memorandum Circular 2012-120 dated July 12, 2012 and to organize, activate,
strengthen and sustain the municipal’s Local Council for the Protection of
Children (LCPC) pursuant to Section 15 of Republic Act No. 9344.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the department head/s or
personnel concerned to comply with the completion and submission to the Audit
Team of the Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation (AAPSI) for Part III
of CY 2018 AAR and on every AAR thereafter.

We recommended the BAC in coordination with the Municipal Engineer comply
strictly with the requirements of semestral submission of the PMR pursuant to
Section 12.2 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 and GPPB Circular no. 03-15.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant set-up and maintain Special
Accounts in the General Fund for the monitoring of operating performance and
viability of the Municipality’s economic enterprises and 20% Development Fund,
and to serve as a decision making tool of the agency. Also, record all transactions
pertaining to these economic enterprises to the corresponding subsidiary ledgers.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and
the Municipal Treasurer to evaluate the validity of the long outstanding payables
and seek authority from the Sangguniang Bayan for the reversion of the long
outstanding payables to Unappropriated Surplus to reflect the correct balance of
payables as of year-end, in pursuant to Republic Act (RA) No. 3526 as
implemented by Executive Order (EO) No. 109, s.1993.
The Municipal Accountant to review the composition of the accounts under the
Due to NGAs and identify the appropriate classification for those items identified
in the audit and remit to the source agency the balances and/or to be reverted in
the General Fund for appropriation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and
the Municipal Treasurer to exhaust efforts to obtain sufficient information on
those dormant accounts. Perform the required analysis of dormant accounts by
conducting the regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the
existence of the receivables and determine the concerned debtors or accountable
officers to notify them of their outstanding balance. Otherwise, if they can no
longer be traced, expedite the submission of the request for write-off of these
dormant accounts to the Commission on Audit supported with Sangguniang
Bayan Resolution requesting the same and certification as to extent of
management validation on these accounts, aging schedule and available records
pertaining to these dormant accounts, if any.

(a) Requiring the Municipal Mayor prioritize the completion of physical count and disposal of unserviceable Municipal properties acquired before 2011 to the Inventory Committee for reference; (b) Directing the Inventory Committee to continue the count of all other PPE items until completed and reconcile results with the accounting records; (c) compelling the use of stock cards in the General Service Office to facilitate better monitoring and easier reconciliation; (d) requiring the OIC-Municipal Accountant to check the variances noted in the PPE schedule and accordingly effect adjustments; (e) requiring the updated management inventory of unserviceable PPE and order their immediate disposal in accordance with the applicable procedures on supply and property management in local government units.
observations.managementAction: The GSO is already using stock cards to facilitate better monitoring and easier reconciliation. The Municipality Inventory Committee has started conducting the physical inventory of all other PPE items until completed and reconcile results with the accounting staff. The OIC-Municipal Accountant has started checking the variances noted. The management has already submitted the updated inventory of unserviceable PPE and ordered their immediate disposal in accordance with the applicable procedures on supply and property management in local government units.
observations.reason: Due to incomplete data/information for the inventory of properties acquired before 2011 the inventory of properties was not completely turn-over to the Inventory Committee. Lack of records and inadequate inventory staff. Adjustments were not yet effected by the accounting office. Disposal of the unserviceable properties was only partially done.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:  Require the Municipal Accountant and the GSO to reconcile the records of motor vehicles being maintained by their respective offices for fair presentation of Motor Vehicles account in the financial statements;  Require the GSO to re-claim the unit of motorcycle donated to the Municipality that was turned over by the Municipal Police Office to the Batangas Provincial Police Office;  Document every donated asset by executing a deed of donation and acceptance, duly signed by the donor and the donee, reflecting thereon the type of asset being donated, the related costs, the intent for the use of the said donated assets and to furnish the GSO and Municipal Accountant with copies thereof for recording purposes;  Cause the immediate disposal of all unserviceable/junked vehicles through proper disposal procedures of government property pursuant to COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the GSO have started to reconcile the records of motor vehicles maintained by each office. The GSO has started updating the records for the unserviceable properties and completed an updated inventory of unserviceable PPE. The GSO has not yet re-claimed the donated motorcycle unit; documents for the donated vehicles were not yet prepared.
observations.reason: Reconciliation between the accounting and GSO was not yet completed due to limited time and manpower. The GSO has not yet re-claimed the donated motorcycle due to ongoing reconciliation between the GSO and Batangas Police Office. Disposal was only partially done.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to provide additional manpower to the Municipal Accounting Office for the need to establish the subsidiary ledgers per taxpayer. Direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct a detailed review of the RPT and SET accounts to determine the cause of the differences as to why and effect the necessary adjustments.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer have started to reconcile the RPT and SET accounts to determine the causes of the differences and effect the necessary adjustments to the accounts to be in consonance with the treasurer’s records, however budgetary constraints limited immediate full implementation.
observations.reason: Budgetary constraints affected the immediate establishment of the subsidiary ledgers and full reconciliation; adjustments to the accounts were started but not fully effected.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:  the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit to the Municipal Accounting Office the List of Unreleased Bank Checks in time for the closing of the books to enable the reversion back to cash in bank and payable accounts the amount of unreleased checks;  the Municipal Treasurer to send written notice to the payees for the existence of checks and require submission to facilitate issuance of new checks for the checks which have become stale; and  the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments of reconciling items determined during the preparation of the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements on a timely basis for an accurate and reliable Cash in Bank account balance in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made the necessary adjustments. The Municipal Treasurer prepared and submitted the List of Unreleased Checks in time for closing of the books and sent notices where applicable; necessary adjustments were effected.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the ICO-Municipal Accountant observe the requirements in the preparation of the financial statements in accordance with the PPSASs and the requirements in the preparation of the pro-forma Notes to Financial Statements prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016 to assist users in understanding the financial statements and to provide them with information relevant to decision-making.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal management has complied with the recommendation and observed the disclosure requirements in the preparation of the financial statements and notes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to (a) reconcile employees’ records at GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG to identify the unremitted balances and immediately remit the amounts due to these agencies; and (b) identify the cause/s of the under remittance for some accounts and make the proper adjustments. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers of the Due to GSIS and Due to Pag-IBIG accounts, as required by COA Circular No. 2015-009.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has started reconciling the accounts and records at GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG to identify the unremitted balances and to remit the amounts due. Subsidiary ledgers were not yet fully maintained by the Accounting Department due to limited staff.
observations.reason: Due to lack of staff, the subsidiary ledgers were not yet maintained by the accounting office; reconciliation is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer and other concerned officials to submit at the beginning of the year the lists of all on-going government projects/programs/activities ("PPA") and those to be implemented during the year, and thereafter, every quarter in compliance with the above cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted at the beginning of the year the lists of all on-going government projects/programs/activities and those to be implemented for the year, in accordance with the cited regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the honorable members of the Sanggunian include among their top priorities the completion of the building initially funded by the Provincial Government to accommodate the storeroom and other offices of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor had provided the storeroom for the financial documents of the Municipality. The Municipality submitted the necessary documents and coordinated with the Provincial Government regarding the completion of the building.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management:  strictly comply with the requirement of Section 21 of R.A. 10121 in the determination of the minimum amount to be set aside as LDRRMF, which should not be less than 5% of the estimated revenue from regular sources;  ensure that the unutilized amount of LDRRMF be transferred from the General Fund to the Special Trust Fund at year-end to guarantee the availability of funds for disaster risk reduction and management activities of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council within the five-year validity period; and  provide the LDRRM Officer with at least three trained and qualified staff for the effective and efficient disaster risk reduction and management.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal management had complied with the requirement to set aside LDRRMF; the Municipality transferred the unutilized LDRRMF amount of the prior year to the Special Trust Fund for the year 2017. The municipality provided additional staff for disaster risk reduction and management. The LDRRMO, Engineer and Council have started to prepare plans and procurement activities.
observations.reason: The balance of LDRRMF for CY 2018 was transferred to the LDRRMF Special Trust Fund in 2019. Some staffing and planning activities remained incomplete at year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRM Council expedite procurement of planned completed infrastructure projects and equipment; incorporate in the mandatory LDRRM Investment Plan the utilization of the prior year’s unexpended balance and savings from completed Capital Outlay; undertake vulnerability assessment, hazard mapping and capacity building research activities to identify high disaster risk areas in the Municipality and plan and implement necessary mitigating projects and activities; and strengthen rescue and recovery facilities, manpower and equipment to ensure readiness in case of urgency.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMO, Engineer and MPDC have incorporated in the investment plan the utilization of the prior year’s unexpended balance and savings. The LDRRMO has started to assess and undertake research activities to identify high risk areas; rescue and recovery facilities, manpower and equipment are being assessed and enhanced.
observations.reason: Some activities and procurement were not completed within the year due to delayed implementation; rescue and recovery facilities and equipment readiness remained incomplete.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:  direct the Municipal Engineer to cause the immediate implementation of the allocated projects to achieve their purpose and maximize the value of the fund; and  through the Municipal Development Council, evaluate and study the feasibility of the projects including those with continuing appropriations to ensure delivery of intended services to the public and require specification in the plan as to the quarter on which projects are to be implemented.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer has coordinated with the MPDC to evaluate and implement the priority projects; several projects were implemented in 2018 and additional projects were scheduled for implementation. The Municipal Development Council evaluated the projects and studies were undertaken for feasibility.
observations.reason: Not all projects were implemented in 2017 due to planning and manpower constraints; some projects were implemented only in 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the department head/s or personnel concerned to comply with the completion and submission to the Audit Team of the Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation (AAPSI) for Part III of CY 2017 AAR and on every AAR thereafter.
observations.managementAction: The management had already complied with the recommendation and submitted the AAPSI.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC in coordination with the Municipal Engineer comply strictly with the requirements of semestral submission of the PMR pursuant to Section 12.2 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 and GPPB Circular No. 03-15.
observations.managementAction: The BAC had complied with the recommendation for CY 2019 and submitted the required PMR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant set-up and maintain Special Accounts in the General Fund for the monitoring of operating performance and viability of the Municipality’s economic enterprises and 20% Development Fund, and to serve as a decision making tool of the agency. Also, record all transactions pertaining to these economic enterprises to the corresponding subsidiary ledgers.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has already started the setting up and maintaining the special accounts under the General Fund in CY 2019 and to record the transactions pertaining to the economic enterprises to the corresponding subsidiary ledgers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to evaluate the validity of the long outstanding payables and seek authority from the Sangguniang Bayan for the reversion of the long outstanding payables to Unappropriated Surplus to reflect the correct balance of payables as of year-end, in pursuant to RA No. 3526 as implemented by EO No. 109, s.1993. The Municipal Accountant to review the composition of the accounts under the Due to NGAs and identify the appropriate classification for those items identified in the audit and remit to the source agency the balances and/or to be reverted in the General Fund for appropriation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer have already evaluated the validity of the outstanding payables and they already made a request from the Sangguniang Bayan for an authority for the reversion of the long outstanding payables to Unappropriated Surplus. The Municipal Accountant is in the process of reviewing the composition of the accounts under Due to NGAs for evaluation of each item and to make the necessary adjustments, if warranted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to exhaust efforts to obtain sufficient information on those dormant accounts. Perform the required analysis of dormant accounts by conducting the regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of the receivables and determine the concerned debtors or accountable officers to notify them of their outstanding balance. Otherwise, if they can no longer be traced, expedite the submission of the request for write-off of these dormant accounts to the Commission on Audit supported with Sangguniang Bayan Resolution requesting the same and certification as to extent of management validation on these accounts, aging schedule and available records pertaining to these dormant accounts, if any.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer together with the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer commented that they continue exerting effort to obtain information on the dormant accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor designate members of the
Inventory Committee that could focus on conducting a thorough, complete and
independent physical count of all Municipal real properties and semi-expendable
items. Direct them to prepare PPE property cards, and reconcile results of
inventory with the accounting records to arrive at the proper balance of the PPE
accounts. Put property tags on all PPE items issued to employees of the
municipality as form of control and reference when conducting
inspection/inventory. Considering these findings on PPE as a perennial issue of the
Municipality, we recommended that these procedures be done by account until all
PPE accounts in the financial statements are completed by year-end.

We also recommended that he require periodic inventory report to monitor
the status of their count and the submission of the annual inventory report not later
than January 31 of the following year for approval, a copy furnished to the COA
Auditor.

Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant check the variances
noted in the PPE schedule and submit the reconciled copies to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw the Journal Entry
Vouchers for recognizing the ongoing projects as assets under the Construction in
Progress account in the General Fund books by the amount of completed portion and/or the completed projects under the appropriate PPE account for fair
presentation of the Statement of Financial Position.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Maunicipal
Accountant and other concerned department heads to assess and determine the
probability of collection and possibility of claims for such long outstanding
receivables and payables of the Municipality. If after all efforts and means available
have been exhausted but collection and payment remains improbable, request for
write-off of the receivables while revert the payables in accordance to procedures
prescribed in COA Cireular 2016-005.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
 e the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the list of unreleased checks at the
end of the year and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for
reversion back to cash in bank and payable accounts in order to fairly
present the cash in bank and other affected accounts in the financial
statements;

 e the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments of
reconciling items determined during the preparation of the Monthly
Bank Reconciliation Statements on a timely basis for an accurate and
reliable Cash in Bank account balance in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Engineer to explain in writing the prolonged suspension of work givem the
unjustifiable reasons. Cite and submit documentation for the determination of the
grounds for suspension and the evaluation of absence of such ground upen ordering
resumption of work. Likewise, submit the PERT-CPM and explain inconsistencies
in the number of labor days as shown in the bidding documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources
Officer to prioritize the repair of the available equipment/procurement of necessary
equipment, expedite the relocation of the informal settlers around Municipal MRF,

and require the barangay officials to restore the Barangay Materials Recovery
Facility and closely monitor solid waste management in the barangays.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor establish the Municipal
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office composing of the designated
Municipal DRRM Officer and at least three staff for the effective and efficient
disaster risk reduction planning and management. Prioritize the purchase of rescue
vehicle and other planned infrastructure projects necessary to mitigate the disaster
risk related to such projects.

We also recommended the LDRRM Council to convene and perform
improved assessment of the conditions of barangays and other parts of the
territorial area of the Municipality to identify high disaster risk areas and
accordingly plan and implement necessary mitigating projects or activities.
Strengthen the rescue and recovery facilities, manpower and equipment to ensure
readiness in case of urgency.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Engineer to cause the immediate implementation of the projects that had been
allocated funds in order to fully attain the purpose and intent for establishing the
fund at the soonest possible time, thereby, maximizing the real value of the money
on hand.

As already cited in the previous AAR, we again recommended the Municipal
Mayor to require the imposition of liquidated damages against the suppliers who
were delayed in delivery. We also recommended the General Service Office to be
vigilant of delayed deliveries and immediately inform in writing the Municipal
Accounting Office in order to facilitate appropriate deduction of liquidated
damages from any payment due to the supplier at fault.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:

a. the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) Executive Committee and its
Technical Working Group (TWG) to generate gender statistics, sex-
disaggregated data and other gender-related information from primary
and secondary sources such as surveys and studies as guided by PCW-
DILG- DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01 to
48

determine gender issues surfacing in the locality for proper planning,
programming and policy formation for GAD;

b. the GFPS-TWG prepare a GAD Plan based on established gender issues,
specify the cause and objective and the funds allocated to finance each
and every activity and submit the same to DILG and to the Provincial
Planning and Development Office for review and to ensure the alignment
of the municipal GAD PPAs with the priorities of the province. See to it
that planned activities are geared towards the attainment of gender
equality and women’s empowerment and ensure full utilization of the
fund to fully address gender issues.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned officials
to refund the amounts spent totalling $39,824.00 for attendance to said seminar. We
also recommend the Management to file appropriate charges against the erring
officials.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer be prudent in checking documentary attachments submitted to their
office.

We recommended that the Local School Board deliberate and review the
SEF budget with emphasis on the proper identification, prioritization and selection
of projects suitable to the actual needs of each of the public schools in the
Municipality. Consider including other matters essential to the provision of holistic
education such as materials, equipment, and the like as provided in DECS, DBM
and DILG Joint Circular No. 01, s. 1998 dated April 14, 1998, depending on the
actual necessity.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Engineering Office to prioritize any infrastructure projects that would be identified
and funded by SEF for the benefits of the schools in the municipality.
